content: PyTorch MPS backend has documented correctness bugs beyond simple precision loss: incorrect handling of scalar index tensors in index_select, incorrect torch.var on zero-dimensional inputs, incorrect AvgPool2d outputs, zeroing of imaginary parts in complex tensor ops, and reports of measurably worse training/inference numerical results on MPS vs CPU/CUDA for the same float32 code and weights, traced to MPS using different low-level kernels per-op than CPU/CUDA (not just float64-unsupported). Implication for SAM2-on-Mac: do not assume bit-for-bit or even close numerical parity with published CUDA benchmarks/reference masks — mask boundaries and confidence scores may differ subtly by device backend, so any acceptance-threshold tuning (e.g., IoU cutoffs) should be done empirically on-device (MPS), not copied from a CUDA reference.
